RECORD TABLE 
 
When the quality of the products changes, the QuID is updated and a row is added 
to this table.  The third column specifies which sections or sub-sections have been 
updated.  The fourth column should mention the version of the product to which the 
change applies.

Issue 2.0 – November 2025 
1) INTRODUCTION 
a) Product Name  
 
OMI_CLIMATE_ThSL_GLOBAL_area_averaged_anomalies and 
OMI_CLIMATE_THSL_GLOBAL_trend. 
 
b) Indicator Evaluation Method 
 
The calculation of steric height between two depth layers h(z 1,z2) involves a vertical 
integration of ocean density ρ, which in turn can be estimated from temperature T 
and salinity S measurements, and the ocean pressure p (ρ(T,S,p)):  
,  
 
where ρ0 is a reference density (Tomzcak and Godfrey, 1994). Steric height h(z 1,z2) has 
the dimension of height and is expressed in meters (see also von Schuckmann et al., 
2009). Annual mean values are given here.  
Area averaged time series for thermosteric sea level and their error bars are 
calculated for the region Near-global Ocean (60°S-60°N)

catalog_entry.summary: This Quality Information Document for OMI_CLIMATE_ThSL provides details on the evaluation method, scientific validation, and input products used in calculating thermosteric sea level trends.
